es.literal: to pay with the same coin
es.meaning: To pay someone back in kind; to retaliate the same way.
es.example: Me ignoró, así que le pagué con la misma moneda.
es.exampleTranslation: He ignored me, so I paid him back in kind.

it.literal: to give back bread for focaccia
it.meaning: To pay someone back in kind — usually with interest.
it.example: Mi ha fatto uno sgarbo e io gli ho reso pan per focaccia.
it.exampleTranslation: He slighted me and I gave as good as I got.
it.regionNote: Submitted as 'vendere pan per focaccia' glossed 'pass something off as better than it is'. Web-verified 2026-07-16 against De Mauro/Treccani/Wikipedia: the canonical verb is RENDERE and the meaning is repaying a wrong with equal or GREATER harshness (Boccaccio, Decameron VIII.8). The submitted gloss matches the ETYMOLOGY (the two breads' unequal worth; originally even a positive neighborly-exchange sense) but not modern usage. A human should still open the De Mauro page before approval — web verification is not the sign-off.

bridge.note: Both settle scores in kind, but the ledgers differ: Spanish pays you back con la misma moneda — exact change — while Italian hands back bread where you gave focaccia: repayment with INTEREST, the coarser loaf for the finer. Italian even has the flat calque 'pagare con la stessa moneta'; the idiomatic form is the older, harsher one.

interference.es_to_it: Calquing gives 'pagare con la stessa moneta' — real Italian, but flat; the idiomatic form is rendere pan per focaccia, and it threatens MORE than you got, not the same. Note the clipped 'pan' inside the fixed phrase.
interference.it_to_es: Calquing 'devolver pan por focaccia' is not Spanish. Pay them back con la misma moneda.

review.notes: Submitted by Joe 2026-07-16 from his Italian teacher contact as 'vendere...'. Verb and meaning corrected and web-verified same day (see it.regionNote). Teacher's gloss traced to real etymology — an interesting error, not a careless one. IT reviewer: confirm 'with interest' nuance vs exact-change 'misma moneda'.
